title: calophyllum waliense
intro: Calophyllum waliense, also known as Bintangor or Borneo camphor tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Calophyllaceae, native to Borneo and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are derived from its essential oils and resin. The health benefits of Calophyllum waliense include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of ointments, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
